title: ranunculus repens f. glabratus
intro: Ranunculus repens f. glabratus, also known as creeping buttercup, is a flowering plant species within the Ranunculus genus that is characterized by its white or pale yellow flowers and yellow-green foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of ranunculin, a toxic compound that has been shown to have medicinal properties. The health benefits of Ranunculus repens f. glabratus include its ability to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of a tincture, salve, or cream, although it is essential to note that proper identification, preparation, and dosing are crucial due to the plant's toxic properties.
